summary: The package root export face is holed: `kind()`s own hook and facts types are unreachable from it, and `globValid` — 1 of 24 predicate constructors — never re-exports

new.description: New: the export face gets a home that holds it MECHANICALLY, not a hand-list that re-rots on the next added type — the field report's own argument (an exported signature naming an unexported type just moves the trap one level down). Load `typescript` (already a devDependency) and walk the emitted `dist/src/index.d.ts`, which exists at test time (`pnpm --dir sdk test` runs `tsc -p` before `node --test`): assert every type a root-exported declaration names is itself root-exported. Precedent for asserting over the root's export surface exists at `sdk/test/emit.test.ts:623` (`import * as sdk`, asserting removed child-kind constructors are `undefined`) — that is a negative one-off inside an emit test, not an export-surface home; name the consideration in the commit body per engineering.md. **The seam half is out of scope and excluded by the walk**: `Declarations`/`PayloadMember` and the generated row family they reach are routed to the `(seam-rows-public-face)` fork — the walk excludes `src/generated/**` and says so, never silently. If the mechanical walk proves infeasible in one tick, the honest fallback is an enumerated type-import test PLUS a stated gap in the commit body — never a silent hand-list.

edit.description: The root export face (the `./kind.js` type block at 54-71, the `./contract.js` block at 26-50, the `./emit.js` block at 90). Six types are named by root-exported signatures yet never re-export — verified on disk, the closure the field report asked for rather than the pair it reported: `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberValue` (kind.ts:384), named by `KindDefinition.render` (226), `KindOptions.render` (276) and `EmbeddedMemberValue.render` (340); `EdgeTargetFacts` (367) and `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberCollectionEntry` (353), named by that type's `targets` (399) and `collections` (392); `KindOptions` (275), the second parameter of the root-exported `kind()` (286); `Template` (120), named by the root-exported `KindFacts.templates` (163); `SettingsResidue` (emit.ts:601), named by the root-exported `EmitResult.settings`. Plus one VALUE hole in the same face: `globValid` (contract.ts:116) is the only one of the 24 predicate constructors absent from the root block — used solely by `builtins.ts` (503/595), so an author cannot spell `clause(globValid("paths"), …)` over their own kind without deep-importing. The predicate vocabulary is closed and the enum in code is the authority (`contract.md`, "clause"); an author-facing vocabulary 23/24 wide silently disagrees with it.

Line 37 deep-imports `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberValue` from `../src/kind.js` while line 36 imports the root — temper's own test suite reproduces the exact consumer trap the field report names, which is the cheapest available proof the hole is real and not theoretical. Flip that import to `../src/index.js` (the render-hook fixture at 1164 is its consumer). Behavior unchanged; the existing tests are the coverage.

tests.asserts: every type a root-exported declaration names is itself root-exported (walked mechanically over the emitted `index.d.ts`, the seam's generated row family excluded), and `globValid` is reachable from the root beside its 23 sibling predicate constructors
the render-hook fixture types its parameter from the package root, no deep import into `src/kind.js`

acceptance: A consumer types a `render` hook parameter, a `kind()` options bag, a nesting `Template`, and a `globValid` clause off `@dtmd/temper` alone — no deep import into dist internals (pipeline.md, "The SDK": the authoring surface is typed at the keystroke, and a type the author cannot import defeats that). The closure is held by a test, not a hand-list. `pnpm --dir sdk test` green. **The closure is the ROOT face only, and the `claude-code` subpath is deliberately NOT widened — the c6de9b0 addendum's ask, declined with its reason on the record.** distribution.md ("What ships — three channels") splits the package: kernel nouns at the root, the first-party provider face — built-in kinds and default contracts as exported values — at the subpath. A `render` hook's parameter type is a kernel authoring noun, not a provider value. And the trap the addendum fears cannot form: `kind()` is root-only (`sdk/src/claude-code.ts` re-exports built-ins, default contracts and the prose constructors, no constructor from `./kind.js`), so a consumer declaring the posture kind whose hook this types already imports the root — naming the type beside the `kind()` that needs it is the designed shape, never a reach-back. If a consumer ever authors a kind from the subpath alone, that is the condition that reopens this.
notes: Routes the 07-16 centercode field report (filed 9adefde) and the PR #22 dedup addendum (c6de9b0). Gap re-verified on disk at 420da04, unmoved since `observed at 58efe11`. Scoped to the VERIFIED closure, not the reported pair: 2 types reported, the walk found 6 plus the `globValid` value hole. The addendum's "both public faces" ask is DECLINED, reason in acceptance, never silently dropped. The seam half is the `(seam-rows-public-face)` fork. scoped at 3fdebb8.

summary: An embedded kind's declared edge fields never reach the lock's assembly `edge` facts, so an embedded edge resolves to nothing and every `degree({incoming})` clause over it reads 0

edit.description: The root cause, verified on disk. `compileDeclarations` (695) computes ONE kind list at 696 — `unitKindsInPlay(kindsInPlay(harness))` — and spends it twice: on `kinds.map(kindFactRow)` (705), where the embedded filter is CORRECT (an embedded kind takes no kind-fact row; it reaches the lock through its host's `templates` column alone), and on `assemblyFactRows(harness, kinds)` (708), where it is not. `unitKindsInPlay` (278-282) drops every `locus.kind === "embedded"` kind, and `assemblyFactRows` (323-331) writes one `{fact: "edge", from, field, to}` row per `kind.edgeFields` — so an embedded kind's declared edge fields silently produce NO assembly edge fact. Split the two lists: the fact rows keep `unitKindsInPlay`, the assembly edge facts range over `kindsInPlay` (every kind in play, at any locus — the comment at 258 already says that is what the set means). The edge is a declared relationship at any grain, so the row is owed whatever the source's locus.

The reader half of the same seam. With the edge fact present, `resolved_edges` (792-820) still resolves nothing: it collects `targets` as the bare `features.id` of every member of `edge.to` (801-807) and compares them against whatever `edge_targets` (1081-1087) reads out of the source's field. An embedded member's edge leaf is a FULL `kind:name` address, not a bare id — `sdk/src/kind.ts:370` pins it ("The target's `kind:name` address — what the edge field's leaf authored") and `sdk/src/emit.ts:248-274` resolves it that way — so `targets.contains("skill:data-access")` is false and the edge drops, silently, before any degree count. Normalize the spelling at read time: accept a bare identity OR a `kind:name` address whose kind component is `edge.to`, and resolve by identity within the target kind either way — one resolution path, the spec's own words, and the read-time-normalization precedent decision 0024 set for the lock's own labels (`pipeline.md`, "The lock"). A `kind:` prefix naming any kind but `edge.to` stays dangling and keeps its existing finding.

The assembly-row assertion home — `declarations.assembly` is asserted at 260/275/279/285, so the new row proof belongs beside them rather than in a second home (engineering.md, "One job, one home"). Assert an embedded kind declaring an edge field writes its assembly `edge` fact, and that the kind still takes no kind-fact row — the two halves 696 conflates, pinned apart so a future filter cannot silently re-merge them.

The end-to-end reference-graph acceptance home (its own header: route resolution against a lock-declared edge field, driving the real binary). Add the embedded case: a host member whose embedded child carries an address-spelled edge leaf at a lock-declared edge fact resolves, and a `degree({incoming})` bound over the target counts it — the reach clause the field report watched read 0.

schemaDelta: none — no built-in declares `edgeFields` (`sdk/src/builtins.ts` carries none) and `src/builtin_lock.toml` holds no assembly `edge` row, so the embedded lock does not re-derive.

tests.asserts: an embedded kind's declared edge field writes an assembly `edge` fact row, while the kind itself still takes no kind-fact row
an embedded member's address-spelled edge leaf resolves to its target and counts toward that target's incoming degree

acceptance: A field-declared embedded edge resolves on the one path every other edge resolves on and counts for degree exactly as a frontmatter field's does (contract.md, "edge": a field edge is declared at any grain — a frontmatter field, an embedded member's edge field — and one resolution path and one degree semantics hold). A consumer's reach clauses judge embedded citations without a leaf-mention shim. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 centercode regression (filed 3fdebb8), diffed forward from `observed at 8b43293` over 5c3a618/2722ca2/28ad61f: unmoved. Root cause is narrower than the report's "0025 hasn't landed": one kind list reused for two jobs at declarations.ts:696. 5c34ced's fence test hand-writes the embedded edge fact (gate_fail_loud.rs:279), pinning the engine and never this seam — `format-places-edges` is unreachable through real emit for the same reason; restored here. scoped at 3fdebb8.

summary: A body-shaped clause bound to an embedded kind decides nothing and says nothing: the embedded features lift zeroes the body, so `max_lines` reads 0 lines and passes over any value

edit.description: `embedded_member_features` (1594-1631) is where the silence is made: it lifts a `nested_member` row's leaves into `Features` as string fields and hard-codes `body_lines: 0`, `headings: Vec::new()`, `sections: Vec::new()` (1611-1613) — honest, since an embedded member owns no document (its own doc comment at 1586 says so), but it means every body-shaped predicate reads an empty body. The third dispatcher (898) then runs those features through `validate` exactly as at-locus members, so the clause evaluates and passes. Pass the bound kind's locus to the fence below at this dispatch — `declared_embedded_kinds` (1485) already names the set.

The fence. `Predicate::MaxLines` judges `features.body_lines <= *max` (672) — `0 <= 2` over a 15-line value, silent. The same zeroed lift makes `RequireSections` (678), `MustDefine` (693), `SectionContains` (701) and `NameMatchesDir` (721) undecidable over an embedded member. `vacuities` (159) takes the predicate ALONE — its doc says so: "a predicate that can never decide anything over *any* selection" — and a body-shaped predicate IS decidable over an at-locus selection, so the existing fence cannot see this. Widen it to take the bound kind's locus alongside the predicate, and fence a body-shaped predicate bound to an embedded kind as inadmissible, naming the predicate and the kind. The judge-less fence at 145 (`judgeless`) is the shape precedent, and 28ad61f's `Facet` retirement is the caution: the parameter earns its place only if nothing else re-splits on it.

The fail-loud home, and already the home of the embedded-kind clause dispatch proof (`a_clause_bound_to_an_embedded_kind_judges_its_members`, 307). Add the fence's two halves beside it: a `max_lines` bound to an embedded kind fails admissibility loud rather than passing over a long value, and the embedded kind's decidable clauses (`format-places-edges`, a field predicate over a leaf) still judge — the fence must not swallow the dispatch 5c34ced shipped.

tests.asserts: a body-shaped clause (`max_lines`) bound to an embedded kind is an admissibility error naming the predicate and the kind, and an embedded kind's decidable clauses still judge their members

acceptance: A clause an embedded kind's members can never decide fails admissibility instead of passing silently (contract.md, "well-formedness": the contract is coherent before it judges anything — no vacuous clause, every finding an error; intent.md invariant 6: no path silently degrades). `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 centercode probe (filed 3fdebb8), diffed forward from `observed at 8b43293`: unmoved. The report's diagnosis is wrong; scoped to the verified mechanism instead. 5c34ced's dispatch DOES reach composed nested members (main.rs:898) — the clause IS judged, against a body zeroed at the lift, so it passes. Vacuity, not a missing judge: the fence belongs at admissibility, never a second dispatch. scoped at 3fdebb8.

summary: The built-in adoption: `skill` templates its bundled reference documents at the directory's markdown, and `supporting-doc` ships as their prose-only kind

edit.description: `skill` (142-158) declares no template. Give it its bundled reference documents as one file-child layer — `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16). New `supporting-doc` kind beside it: fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename, nested-file locus. Its default contract ships **empty** per the standing bar ("Default contracts"); its one clause is SUPPORTING-DOC-REACH-CLAUSE's to add once an incidence clause outside a requirement is admissible, never this entry's to invent. The kind ships without joining the surface enumeration, the `requirement` precedent. Rider (open-questions): the doc-comment cites at 565/611 name two deleted `packages/{rule,memory}.anthropic/PACKAGE.md` files — discharge them while here.

The engine-side reader mirror of the shipped kinds (the `CustomKind::new` calls and `definitions`): `skill`'s row grows its template, and `supporting-doc` enters as the child kind a discovered document is read as — 91c288c landed the `CustomKind::nested_file` constructor (src/kind.rs:600) this row is the first built-in caller of. Rider (open-questions): the narration cites here are dated @07-07 / @07-10 or undated (85, 194/222, 63/106) — every fact still holds; re-verify against the live docs and bump the dates while here.

The embedded built-in lock re-derives once `skill`'s row carries a template and `supporting-doc` has a row — the first built-in to declare either (91c288c left this file byte-identical because no built-in declared the locus; that changes here).
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the two grown rows.
`skill`'s declaration row carries its reference-document template, and `supporting-doc`'s row carries its nested-file locus and empty field schema.

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `skill`'s kind row grows its `templates` column (child kind `supporting-doc`, the markdown path pattern), and `supporting-doc` enters as a new kind row at the nested-file locus.

tests.asserts: `skill` declares one file-child layer of `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, and `supporting-doc` is fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically from the SDK with the two grown rows
a real skill directory's markdown document is discovered as that skill's `supporting-doc` child

acceptance: A skill's bundled reference documents are nested file members of the same skill, typed `supporting-doc` (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"), their path the skill template's declared fact — nesting-is-model-containment / locus-is-serialization stops being an unbacked claim. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 3 — the built-in adoption; 0027 says it re-enters buildable, and it does, minus the clause. The template claims the honest markdown subset: supporting files of other types stay unmodeled and named as such (0027's second Rejected bullet), the `settings.json` partial-governance posture. Discharges the `supportingDocs()` factory asymmetry in open-questions. Gate re-tested at b745415: NESTED-FILE-DISCOVERY unshipped. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: The fixture-harness wiring builder belongs in `tests/common`, not copy-pasted per suite: three copies of tmpdir → `.temper` → `harness.ts` → `vendor_sdk` collapse into one home

edit.description: The declared one home for test scaffolding — its own header (1-4) names "the SDK vendoring used by tests that drive a real `node` subprocess" as the copy-per-suite it exists to end. It carries every ingredient (`tmpdir` 31, `scaffold` 44, `vendor_sdk` 96) and not the builder that composes them into a fixture harness, which is exactly the surface the three copies below each re-derive. Add that builder: a label plus the fixture program text, returning `(harness, into)`. `tests/install.rs`'s `vendor_sdk` calls are a different job — the install verb scaffolds its own `harness.ts` under test, rather than wiring an authored fixture program — and are out of scope.

`wire_sdk_harness_program` (1223) is the original, and the surface the other two copies' doc comments cite by name as the pattern they follow; `wire_sdk_harness` (1217) binds it to this suite's `HARNESS_PROGRAM`. Retire the local body onto the common home — the `HARNESS_PROGRAM` binding stays suite-local, being this suite's fixture rather than scaffolding.

`wire_memberless_harness` (61-71): a verbatim copy of emit.rs's builder — tmpdir, `.temper`, write `harness.ts`, `vendor_sdk` — whose own doc (58-60) cites `tests/emit.rs`'s `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it follows. Call the common builder with `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM`.

`wire_waypoint_harness` (141, shifted +24 by 91c288c's nested-file-child seam case), the third copy (e76ec85), carrying the same doc cite back to emit.rs's builder. Call the common builder with `WAYPOINT_PROGRAM`.

acceptance: One builder composes tmpdir → `.temper` → `harness.ts` → `vendor_sdk`, in `tests/common`, and no suite re-derives it (engineering.md, "One job, one home": shared fixtures and builders live in one home, never copy-pasted per file). Behavior is unchanged, so the three suites' existing tests are the coverage; `cargo test` green.
notes: Found sweeping 74f4e62..ca4e866: e76ec85 added the third copy, its doc citing emit.rs's `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it followed rather than the home it should have extended — the copy-per-suite `tests/common`'s own header says it exists to end. Serialized behind the chain: shares projection_path_seam.rs with the nested-file work, builtin_lock_frozen.rs with SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS. Re-verified at b745415: all three copies live. rescoped at b745415.

summary: `supporting-doc`'s reach clause: a supporting file is invisible unless its host skill references it — the by-kind universal binding, each-grain incidence, advisory

edit.description: `supporting-doc`'s default contract — the empty exported clause array SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS ships — gains its one clause: an incoming-degree bound over the kind's members, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16), the vendor fact that every supporting file is invisible unless SKILL.md references it. **Universal, not existential**: the fact holds of every supporting document, so the spelling is the by-kind binding with each-grain incidence (`contract.md`, "selection"), never a requirement — a requirement is the opt-in selector and would make a vendor fact consumer ceremony. **Reach is locus-agnostic**: any resolved edge from the host skill counts, post-0025's one edge noun and one degree semantics — a mention included, this clause being the "a contract may" that mention's obligation-free default reserves (`contract.md`, "edge"). Severity **advisory**, per `intent.md` invariant 5: a shipped coverage clause enters advisory, and escalation is the corpus's declared act.
The embedded built-in lock re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains its one clause row.
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the grown clause row.
`supporting-doc`'s default contract carries exactly one clause, at advisory severity, cited and guided — the shipped-clause bar (`builtins.md`, "The clauses live in code").

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains one clause row (the incoming-degree reach bound, advisory).

tests.asserts: `supporting-doc`'s default contract is one advisory reach clause, cited to the vendor fact and carrying its guidance
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically with the clause row

acceptance: A supporting document no edge from its host skill reaches is an advisory finding naming the vendor fact (builtins.md, "Default contracts"; the clause is decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated) — and reach counts any resolved edge, locus-agnostic. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 ruling's clause itself (25e9317 resolved the fork). The single-tag gate names PREDICATE-SELECTION-ALGEBRA — without it an incidence clause outside a requirement is inadmissible, so the clause cannot ship. It ALSO requires SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS: that entry mints the kind and its empty contract, and shares builtins.ts / builtin_lock.toml / builtin_lock_frozen.rs — so this is the queue tail, behind both chains. scoped at d572827.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at b745415: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml:7-9 still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
